title: How to Grill Sliced Halloumi and Watermelon on a BBQ Mat
keyword: grilling halloumi and watermelon on grill mat
meta_description: Learn how to achieve the perfect sear on halloumi and watermelon without the mess. Master grilling halloumi and watermelon on grill mat for a sweet, salty treat
html:
If you've ever tried to grill halloumi or watermelon on open grates, you know the struggle. Halloumi can occasionally slip through the bars, and watermelon—which is mostly water—tends to stick or slump, leaving you with a sugary mess and a flare-up that chars the outside before the inside even warms up.

That is why I swear by grilling halloumi and watermelon on grill mat. It creates a protective barrier that lets the heat through while keeping your food exactly where it belongs. Using BBQ Grill Mats allows you to get those beautiful grill marks without the anxiety of losing a slice of expensive cheese to the charcoal.

Why Use BBQ Grill Mats for Delicate Foods?

Standard grill grates are great for thick steaks, but they are unforgiving with soft or porous foods. Watermelon releases juices as it heats, which drip onto the burners and cause flames to jump up. Halloumi, while it holds its shape better than mozzarella, can still stick to metal surfaces if the temperature isn't perfect.

By using BBQ Grill Mats, you eliminate the sticking and the flare-ups completely. These reusable non-stick mats act as a heat conductor, distributing the temperature evenly across the surface of your food. This means you get a consistent golden-brown sear on your cheese and a caramelized edge on your fruit without the charred, bitter taste of a grease fire.

Preparing Your Halloumi and Watermelon

Preparation is key to ensuring the textures contrast perfectly. For the halloumi, slice the block into rectangles about half an inch thick. Any thinner and they might overcook too quickly; any thicker and you won't get that signature salty crust.

For the watermelon, go for a seedless variety. Cut the melon into wedges or thick discs. I recommend patting the watermelon dry with a paper towel before it hits the grill. Removing excess surface moisture helps the sugars caramelize faster, giving you a better sear when grilling halloumi and watermelon on grill mat.

To elevate the flavor, I suggest a simple brush of olive oil on both the cheese and the fruit. While the mats are non-stick, a light coating of oil enhances the heat transfer and adds a rich finish.

Step-by-Step: Grilling Halloumi and Watermelon on Grill Mat

Follow these steps to get the timing just right, as the cheese and fruit cook at slightly different speeds.

  1. Preheat the Grill: Get your grill to medium-high heat (around 400°F). Once hot, lay your BBQ Grill Mats directly on the grates.
  2. Sear the Halloumi: Place the sliced halloumi on the mat first. Grill for about 2-3 minutes per side. You are looking for a deep golden brown color.
  3. Sear the Watermelon: While the cheese is finishing, add your watermelon slices to the same mat. Grill for 2-4 minutes per side. You want visible grill marks and a slight softening of the flesh, but you don't want the melon to become mushy.
  4. The Final Touch: Remove both from the grill immediately. The residual heat will continue to cook them, so don't overdo it on the grill.

Assembling the Perfect Summer Appetizer

The magic happens when you combine the salty, squeaky texture of the halloumi with the cool, refreshing sweetness of the watermelon. I like to stack them: a slice of watermelon, topped with a piece of grilled halloumi.

To tie it all together, drizzle the platter with a reduction of balsamic glaze or a squeeze of fresh lime juice. A sprinkle of fresh mint leaves or a pinch of red pepper flakes adds a professional touch that transforms this from a simple snack into a gourmet appetizer. Because you used BBQ Grill Mats, the surfaces of the food remain clean and vibrant, making the final presentation look much more polished.

Cleaning and Maintaining Your Grill Mats

One of the best parts about these mats is that they save you from scrubbing the grill grates after every single meal. Since the sugars from the watermelon and the oils from the cheese stay on the mat, your actual grill stays cleaner for longer.

When you're finished, simply let the mat cool down. Most can be wiped clean with a damp cloth and warm soapy water, or tossed in the dishwasher if they are designated as dishwasher-safe. Store them flat to ensure they don't warp, and they'll be ready for your next backyard barbecue.

FAQ

Can I use a grill mat on a gas or charcoal grill?

Yes, BBQ Grill Mats are designed to work on both gas and charcoal grills. They provide the same non-stick benefits regardless of the heat source, though you should always monitor the temperature to ensure you are within the mat's heat rating.

Will the watermelon taste "steamed" instead of grilled?

Not at all. The mats are thin enough to allow the intense heat of the grill to pass through, which creates a sear. Because the mat prevents the food from sticking and tearing, you actually get a cleaner, more consistent grill mark than you would on bare grates.

How long does grilled halloumi stay firm?

Halloumi is unique because of its high melting point. However, it is best served immediately after grilling while it is still warm and soft. As it cools, it will firm up again, but the flavor is most potent right off the grill mat.
